meta.document: BSIR / Homeland Security Grant Program — DFR Relevance
sources: Ohio DFR Pilot Program RFA documentation

programs.name: Homeland Security Grant Program
programs.ael_description: Systems, Small Unmanned Aircraft System (sUAS) — primary DFR AEL item
programs.allocation_method: Allocated to states by DHS formula; states sub-grant to localities
programs.notes: HSGP is one of the most common funding sources for DFR equipment. Agencies apply to their State Administrative Agency (SAA), not directly to FEMA.

programs.name: State Homeland Security Program
programs.purpose: Address statewide all-hazards preparedness, including technology
programs.dfr_eligibility: High — UAS/DFR explicitly covered under AEL 03OE-07-SUAS
programs.who_applies: State agencies apply; sub-grant to local agencies
programs.notes: Ohio DFR Pilot Program funded through ODOT/state mechanism. Other states using SHSP for DFR: CA, TX, FL, MI.

programs.name: Urban Areas Security Initiative
programs.purpose: Enhance regional preparedness in high-risk urban areas
programs.dfr_eligibility: High — major cities with active DFR programs often UASI-funded
programs.notes: Higher award amounts than SHSP. Requires Threat and Hazard Identification and Risk Assessment (THIRA) alignment.

programs.name: Boating Safety Infrastructure and Rescue
programs.purpose: Maritime/waterway safety — less common DFR pathway
programs.dfr_eligibility: Limited — relevant for water rescue, coastal patrol programs
programs.notes: BSIR is narrower than HSGP. More relevant for waterway-adjacent DFR programs (SAR over water).

ineligible_components: Ground-based sensing for DAA (per Ohio DFR RFA precedent — verify with SAA)
platform_verification_path: 1. Confirm platform is not from covered entity (DJI, Autel, Parrot — check current FCC Covered List and NDAA §848 list)
2. For Blue UAS-listed platforms: reference DIU Blue UAS list in grant narrative
3. For non-Blue-UAS NDAA-compliant platforms: provide manufacturer NDAA certification letter
4. Confirm platform is not on FCC Covered List
5. For Parrot ANAFI USA: note French parent company — verify FOCI disclosure requirements with SAA

state_administrative_agencies.description: All HSGP sub-grants flow through the SAA in each state. Agencies must contact their SAA for application timelines and state-specific requirements.
state_administrative_agencies.notes: SAA requirements vary significantly by state. Some states have pre-approved UAS vendor lists (e.g. Ohio). Others allow any NDAA-compliant platform.

application_process.step_2: Confirm state has DFR/UAS in current Investment Justification or THIRA
application_process.step_3: Submit application to SAA (not directly to FEMA)
application_process.step_4: SAA reviews, selects sub-recipients, submits to FEMA
application_process.step_5: FEMA awards to state; state awards to locality
application_process.step_6: Purchase equipment using AEL-compliant items only
application_process.timeline: SAA cycles vary by state — typically annual with applications in Q1–Q2
application_process.reimbursement_note: HSGP is reimbursement-based. Agencies must have budget authority to pay upfront.

key_data_to_track: Annual HSGP appropriation by state (DHS publishes)
State SAA application deadlines (state-by-state, updated annually)
THIRA/SHIRA documents that include UAS/DFR investment justifications
Award announcements (public record — track for program intelligence)
